title: paeonia mascula var. flavescens
intro: Paeonia mascula var. flavescens, also known as the yellow peony, is a variant of the Paeonia mascula species, a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Paeonia mascula var. flavescens include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
